statement.latex: An isometric action of a group$G$on a metric space$S$is called if for every$> 0$there exist$R, N > 0$such that for every two points$x, y$with$d(x, y) R$, there are at most$N$elements$g G$satisfying$d(x, gx)$and$d(y, gy)$.
